text_fields.name: Cyrus Shekari
text_fields.cards[05c7333d-b63a-4129-ab70-b723024a00:
What excites me most is that this role sits exactly at the intersection of AI workflow delivery, cross-functional project ownership, and customer-facing impact - which is where I've spent the past two years. At Microsoft, I built an internal AI agent for drill planning that cut planning cycle time by 39% and increased drill capacity by 21%. That wasn't a research project - it was a production deployment that real teams depended on, and I owned it end to end, from defining intents and prompts through measuring outcomes. Doing that kind of work for Aircall's customers, at scale, is a natural and genuinely compelling next step.

The specific product bet Aircall is making also appeals to me. Bringing voice, AI automation, and CRM integration into a single workspace for SMB customer-facing teams is a hard problem with real business stakes - not a demo. The Forward Deployed Engineering model, where you translate customer workflow challenges into reusable, production-ready solutions and then feed those learnings back into Product and Engineering, is exactly the operating model I find most satisfying. The combination of hands-on delivery, enablement, and roadmap influence is rare in a single role.

I've already done most of what this role requires, just inside a large enterprise. I scaled a 2-person resilience program into a platformized deployment system that ran 45+ annual drills and drove $14M+ in business impact, while building the reusable reference architectures and playbooks that made future iterations faster. That required the same core skills this role asks for - stakeholder alignment across Engineering, Product, and field teams, cross-functional project leadership under executive visibility, and a bias toward building things that outlast any single project.

On the AI and automation side, I built and deployed an AI agent for drill planning, ran enablement sessions that drove adoption of code-generation tooling across 14 Azure service teams, and cut documentation retrieval time by 83% by implementing a semantic search framework - all real, instrumented outcomes. I'm comfortable in APIs, JSON, and lightweight scripting, and I pick up new tools quickly. What I'd bring beyond the technical skills is a track record of turning ambiguous, high-stakes problems into structured, repeatable delivery models - which is exactly what scaling AI deployments across an SMB customer base demands.

options.label: No - I do not/will not require sponsorship, and I am authorized to work for any employer in the US
